title: Prospecting & List Building with AI + Data Tools

questions.question: What is 'waterfall enrichment' in the context of prospect list building?

options.text: Sending emails in a cascading sequence over multiple weeks
A sequential data lookup strategy that checks multiple sources until it finds valid data
A technique for gradually increasing email send volume
A method of scoring leads from highest to lowest priority

questions.explanation: Waterfall enrichment is a sequential data lookup strategy: try Source A for email, if no result try Source B, then Source C. Clay automates this across 75+ providers, stopping when it finds valid data. This dramatically improves coverage beyond any single data source.

questions.question: When building an Apollo search, how many filters should you use in your first pass?

options.text: As many as possible for precision

questions.explanation: The lesson recommends no more than 5 filters in your first pass. Over-filtering leads to zero results. Start with core filters (industry, company size, location) and refine from there.

questions.question: According to the Research Depth Pyramid, how much time should you spend on the top 20% of prospects (score 8-10)?

options.text: No research needed — just send templates

questions.explanation: The Research Depth Pyramid allocates 30+ minutes of manual research for the top 20% (score 8-10), 5 minutes of AI-assisted research for the middle 50% (score 5-7), and template-only with segment personalization for the bottom 30% (score 1-4).

questions.question: Tech stack data from Apollo is 95-99% accurate and should be used to exclude prospects who don't match your criteria.
questions.explanation: False. Apollo's tech stack data is only 60-70% accurate. The lesson warns to use it to prioritize prospects, not to exclude them. Excluding based on inaccurate tech data means you could miss qualified prospects.

questions.question: What is the email decay rate for B2B contacts, and why does it matter for list building?

options.text: 2-3% per month — a 90-day-old list is 6-9% invalid
10% per week — lists are useless after a month
Email decay only affects consumer lists, not B2B

questions.explanation: B2B email addresses decay at 2-3% per month due to job changes, company acquisitions, and role changes. A 90-day-old list will have 6-9% invalid emails, which is why verifying emails before sending (using tools like MillionVerifier) is a critical step.

questions.question: Describe your current prospect research process step by step. How long does it take you to build a list of 50 qualified prospects? Which steps from the lesson's SOP could you adopt to cut that time by 60-80%?
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response describes a concrete current process (even if informal), identifies specific time bottlenecks, and references actionable steps from the lesson's SOP such as ICP-to-filter translation, Apollo search building, waterfall enrichment, and email verification.
